subject: [PATCH 03/14] mm: rename uffd-wp PTE accessors to uffd

2026-04-27.patch_summary: This patch renames kernel-internal names related to the uffd-wp PTE bit, which is reused for marking access-tracking PTEs alongside write-protected ones. The rename affects various header files and source code locations across different architectures. The changes are purely mechanical, with no behavior change intended. This is part of a larger patch series that aims to update userfaultfd functionality.

2026-05-04.patch_summary: This patch renames kernel-internal functions and macros related to the uffd-wp PTE bit, which is reused for access-tracking PTEs in Userfaultfd RWP. The renaming is mechanical and does not change behavior. The changes affect various files across the Linux kernel, including architecture-specific headers, mm and fs directories. The goal of this patch is to update internal names to reflect the new meaning of the uffd-wp bit, which depends on the VMA flag (WP or RWP).
